Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We need to analyze the event log and identify cases with significantly longer total resolution times compared to others Then determine potential root causes presence of escalations long waiting times between activities unnecessary delays before investigation resolution steps etc Then explain and propose insights recommendations

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: First compute total resolution time for each case from Receive Ticket timestamp to Close Ticket timestamp since closure is the final step So we compute duration from Receive to Close

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 101 Receive 2024 03 01 08 00 Close 2024 03 01 10 15 duration 2h15m

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 102 Receive 2024 03 01 08 05 Close 2024 03 02 09 15 total 25h10m

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 103 Receive 2024 03 01 08 10 Close 2024 03 01 09 30 duration 1h20m

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 104 Receive 2024 03 01 08 20 Close 2024 03 02 08 30 total 24h10m

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 105 Receive 2024 03 01 08 25 Close 2024 03 03 09 30 total 49h5m

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Now we have durations 101 2h15m 102 25h10m 103 1h20m 104 24h10m 105 49h5m Clearly 101 103 are short less than 3h 102 and 104 are about 24 to 25h which is much longer 105 is extremely long So 102 104 105 are significantly longer Among them 105 is longest So these three are outliers

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now determine potential root causes Lets examine each cases event sequence and timings between activities Look for long gaps waiting times or escalations

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 101 Receive to Triage 10 min Triage to Assign 10 min Assign to Investigate 40 min Investigate to Resolve 60 min Resolve to Close 15 min No large waiting times No escalation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 102 Receive to Triage 25 min Triage to Assign 30 min Assign to Escalate 2h30 Escalate to Investigate 2h30 Investigate to Resolve 19h Resolve to Close 15 min The major gaps are 2h30 between Assign and Escalate 2h30 between Escalate and Investigate and a huge 19h gap between Investigate and Resolve

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: After investigation maybe the resolution is pending something The log shows Investigate at 14 00 and then no activity until next morning at 09 00 That suggests that after investigation the ticket waited overnight before being resolved Possibly because the support agent finished investigation but resolution required waiting or was scheduled next day

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 103 Receive to Triage 5 min Triage to Assign 15 min Assign to Investigate 15 min Investigate to Resolve 30 min Resolve to Close 15 min All short intervals no escalation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 104 Receive to Triage 40 min Triage to Assign 30 min Assign to Investigate 3h30 Investigate to Resolve 19h Resolve to Close 30 min Notice long gap between Assign and Investigate 3.5h Then after investigation long gap to Resolve 19h

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: So similar pattern as 102 after investigation there is a large overnight delay Also initial triage took 40 min assign to investigate took 3.5h

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 105 Receive to Triage 10 min Triage to Assign 25 min Assign to Investigate 10 min Investigate to Escalate 50 min Escalate to Investigate 28h Investigate to Resolve 19h Resolve to Close 30 min

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: So massive delays after escalation it took 28 hours before the next Investigate Then after that Investigate 19 hours before Resolve So similar pattern after final investigation a 19h delay before resolve Also note that after the first Investigate and escalation there is a huge gap before Level 2 picks up the investigation

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: That suggests that the Level 2 team may be overloaded or there is a bottleneck in handling escalated tickets

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Cases without escalations 101 103 are quick Cases with escalations 102 105 are among the longest but 104 without escalation is also long So escalation alone not the only factor

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The overnight delay after investigation appears in all long cases 102 104 105 regardless of escalation However 101 and 103 which were resolved same day did not have such a gap

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: All long cases have this overnight gap after investigation The short cases had investigation earlier in the day and resolved same day within an hour So a potential factor is the time of day when investigation is completed if it is completed after noon the case ends up being resolved the next day

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This could be because the support team gets a surge of tickets at 8am leading to queuing The short tickets may have been processed quickly while others got delayed because the support team became busy Possibly there is a resource constraint limited number of agents for Level 1 leading to queuing

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The consistent overnight delay between Investigate Issue and Resolve Ticket for cases that are not resolved on the same day suggests that resolution is deferred to the next business day This could be due to shift schedules lack of follow through waiting for external actions or a hidden approval or implementation step

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Potential factors Escalation leads to additional delays especially waiting for Level 2 to start investigation Long waiting times between activities are evident across the long cases After assignment agents take several hours to begin investigation A consistent overnight delay between Investigate and Resolve appears in the long cases

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The combination of these factors results in extended cycle times For 104 the investigation started at 13 00 and then the ticket waited overnight For 102 the ticket escalated and then after investigation waited until next morning For 105 both escalation and the overnight delay compounded the total cycle time

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Root cause 1 Escalation delays The time for Level 2 to start investigation varies and can be very long This suggests that Level 2 resources are either overloaded or there is a lack of clear escalation protocol Recommendation Increase Level 2 capacity implement SLA for escalations or allow Level 1 to directly resolve more issues through better training knowledge base

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Root cause 2 Long waiting times before investigation After assignment agents take several hours to begin investigation This could be due to high ticket volume multitasking or lack of prioritization Recommendation Implement workload balancing prioritize tickets based on age or use automation to assign tickets directly to available agents

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Root cause 3 Overnight delay after investigation Tickets that are investigated in the afternoon are not resolved until the next morning adding significant cycle time Recommendation Extend support hours for resolution tasks or ensure that there is a process to handle resolution within the same day perhaps by having a dedicated resolution team

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The cases that took significantly longer 102 104 105 all started in the morning but experienced delays at various steps So escalation is not the sole factor the overnight delay after investigation appears to be the critical bottleneck
